identifiers.policy_profile: string 'default' for default policy or canonical normalized service name for service override profile; no prefixes/suffixes
identifiers.policy_queue_min_ms: equal to selected profile's queue_min_effective_ms before unit penalties/credits
identifiers.identifier_note: window_signature, outage_signature and queue_digest are TRUNCATED sha1 hex digests — 10, 12 and 10 lowercase hex characters respectively — NOT full-length hashes. Do not emit a 40-char sha1 or a 64-char sha256 for these fields; truncate to the stated length. This is distinct from the summary checksum fields, which are full 64-char sha256 digests.

schemas.summary_layout_contract: all summary_exact_keys must be top-level keys in downtime_summary.json; do not nest these fields under a child object
schemas.source_incident_ids_type: array of unique string incident ids sorted ascending in service_windows and incident_queue; use comma_joined_source_incident_ids only inside signature/checksum payloads

summary_aggregation_contract.recoverability_note: Every summary field except the four in summary_field_recovery is a plain aggregate (sum, count, or maximum) over the exact-matched service_windows.json and incident_queue.jsonl rows per the per-field rules below, so no additional worked example is needed for them: once those two outputs are correct, each summary aggregate is fully determined by this contract. summary_field_recovery covers only the four fields (canonical_incident_count, severity_counts, planned_excluded_count, canonical_input_checksum) that depend on planned incidents and therefore never surface in the window or queue outputs.
summary_aggregation_contract.raw_incident_count: number of rows in the selected input JSON before canonicalization
summary_aggregation_contract.unique_incident_ids: number of distinct str(row['incident_id']) values in the raw input
summary_aggregation_contract.canonical_incident_count: number of canonical deduplicated incidents, including planned incidents
summary_aggregation_contract.service_count: number of service keys in service_windows.json; services represented only by planned incidents are absent
summary_aggregation_contract.severity_counts: counts over all canonical incidents, including planned incidents
summary_aggregation_contract.window_totals_and_longest: all duration, overlap, segment, and billable totals plus longest_window_ms are computed over every service window, including windows not admitted to the queue
summary_aggregation_contract.total_debt_adjusted_dispatchable_downtim: sum debt_adjusted_dispatchable_ms over every service window, including windows not admitted to the queue
summary_aggregation_contract.queued_window_count_and_priority_counts: computed over final incident_queue rows only
summary_aggregation_contract.queue_maxima: max_escalation_score, max_exception_balance_score, max_handoff_pressure_score, max_blackout_pressure_score, max_degrade_pressure_score, max_debt_pressure_score, and max_risk_vector are maxima over final queue rows only; use 0 for an empty queue
summary_aggregation_contract.max_debt_out_ms: maximum debt_out_ms over every service window, including windows not admitted to the queue
summary_aggregation_contract.planned_excluded_count: number of canonical deduplicated incidents whose normalized planned value is true
summary_aggregation_contract.critical_window_count: number of all service windows whose max_severity is critical, whether queued or not

schemas.service_windows_container_contract: service_windows.json is a JSON object (not an array): a flat map from canonical service name to the list of that service's merged window objects. Service keys are sorted ascending; each service's window list is sorted by start_ms ascending. Every window object carries exactly the service_windows_exact_keys fields. Do not wrap the map in metadata keys and do not emit a flat array of windows.
schemas.summary_schema_version_note: Fixed contract constant. Emit `schema_version` verbatim as this string; it is not derived from the incident data and does not vary by input.
schemas.schema_version: Output key name in downtime_summary.json. Its value is the fixed contract constant recorded above as summary_schema_version; emit that string verbatim.

required_keys_at_a_glance._note: Read this first. Full detail for each field is elsewhere in this contract; this block exists so a truncated read still yields the complete key set.

serialization.trailing_delimiter: none: never append LF or | after the final value
serialization.planned_encoding: integer text 1 for normalized true and 0 for normalized false; never JSON true/false

checksums.canonical_input_checksum: LF-join incident_id|service|start_ms|end_ms|severity|planned_int rows for canonical incidents sorted by (service ascending,start_ms ascending,str(incident_id) ascending), then SHA-256
checksums.queue_signature_checksum: join outage_signature values in final queue order with | and no trailing delimiter, then SHA-256
checksums.queue_digest_checksum: join queue_digest values in final queue order with | and no trailing delimiter, then SHA-256
checksums.maintenance_compaction_checksum: LF-join service|start_ms|end_ms rows sorted by service ascending then compacted interval (start_ms,end_ms) ascending, then SHA-256
checksums.exception_compaction_checksum: LF-join service|action|start_ms|end_ms rows sorted by service ascending, action in the explicit order boost then suppress, then compacted interval ascending, then SHA-256
checksums.scoped_compaction_order: handoff, blackout, and degrade rows are LF-joined as service|severity_scope|start_ms|end_ms, sorted by (service,severity_scope) ascending then compacted interval ascending
checksums.handoff_compaction_checksum: SHA-256 of scoped_compaction_order rows from handoff data
checksums.blackout_compaction_checksum: SHA-256 of scoped_compaction_order rows from blackout data
checksums.degrade_compaction_checksum: SHA-256 of scoped_compaction_order rows from degrade data
checksums.policy_checksum: resolve complete normalized policies first; emit the default profile line, then one line for every service_overrides key in ascending order using that service key as profile; serialize each line using policy_line_field_order and |, LF-join without a trailing LF, then SHA-256

summary_field_recovery.note: canonical_incident_count, severity_counts, planned_excluded_count and canonical_input_checksum are computed over ALL canonical deduplicated incidents INCLUDING planned ones. Planned incidents never form service windows, so these four fields cannot be reconstructed from service_windows.json or incident_queue.jsonl and have no other feedback handle. The worked example below isolates them end to end: it exercises duplicate grouping and its tie-break, service-alias folding, severity normalization, and planned coercion (all defined authoritatively in /app/incident/rollout_review_log.md), and pins the exact counts and canonical_input_checksum serialization. raw_incident_count and unique_incident_ids are taken from the raw input before canonicalization and are shown for completeness. policy_checksum is not listed here because the resolved policy fields also drive the queue's scoring and thresholds, so a matching incident_queue already fixes it.

guardrails.source_token_rule: the repaired source code, including comments and docstrings, must not contain /tests, expected_outputs.json, or test_outputs.py; runtime reads/imports from the test tree are also forbidden

semantics_source.notes: This document is the output contract only: file paths, schemas, exact key sets, identifier payloads and checksum encodings. It does NOT define how values are derived.
semantics_source.authority: Normalization, dedupe tie-breaks, interval and overlap semantics, domain ordering, policy resolution, attenuation, the debt ledger, unit and threshold math, scoring, priority rules and queue ordering were settled by the change-advisory board; the authoritative record is /app/incident/rollout_review_log.md. Where an early draft proposal conflicts with a later CAB decision, the later decision wins.

canonical_service_label_scope._note: Scope declaration only. The alias table, the trim/lowercase rules and every other canonicalization value are defined authoritatively in /app/incident/rollout_review_log.md; this entry fixes only WHERE the resulting label is used.
canonical_service_label_scope.rule: Service labels are canonicalized once -- trimmed, lowercased, then folded through the alias table -- and the resulting canonical label is the service identity used EVERYWHERE downstream. It is not confined to canonical counting.
applies_to: service window construction and the window's service key
policy, exception, handoff, blackout and degrade DOMAIN MATCHING
domain compaction, performed on the folded label, BEFORE any overlap milliseconds are computed
queue rows, their thresholds and every checksum derived from the above
canonical_service_label_scope.consequence: Two raw labels that fold to the same canonical label are the same service for compaction and overlap purposes: their policy spans compact together as one domain, and the overlap is computed once against the merged spans. Compacting the raw labels separately and only folding afterwards produces different overlap milliseconds and therefore different durations, queue membership and checksums.

governing_entry_index._note: Navigation aid only. It records WHICH review entries carry the governing rule for each pipeline stage; it does not restate any rule, value, divisor, threshold or rounding direction. Read the named entries in /app/incident/rollout_review_log.md for the rules themselves, and note that entries not listed here are either superseded by a later dated entry or carry no estimator semantics.
governing_entry_index.rounding_warning: Rounding direction is not uniform across the pipeline and is not inferable from any neighbouring divisor, including between siblings in the same family. Each divisor's direction is fixed by its own entry above; CAB-2250 records the map.
governing_entry_index.non_normative_entries: CAB-2240, CAB-2243, CAB-2249 and CAB-2262 concern dashboards, archive ingest, publication and access recertification. They carry no estimator semantics.

worked_pipeline_trace._note: End-to-end verification aid. It records the value each stage produces for a SYNTHETIC two-incident scenario so you can check your implementation stage by stage instead of only against a final checksum. It is not drawn from /app/data/outages.json and does not restate any formula: each stage names the review-log entry that governs it, and the arithmetic stays there.
worked_pipeline_trace.preconditions: Both incidents are unplanned and share one canonical service, so they form two separate windows under the stitch rule. Neither window overlaps any maintenance, exception, handoff, blackout or degrade span in the shipped policy files, so every overlap below is 0 and the attenuation layers pass their input through unchanged. That isolates the debt ledger, which is the stage most implementations get wrong.

window_1.governing: CAB-2210 window construction; CAB-2216 attenuation chain
window_1.note: First window in its service, so the ledger starts empty per CAB-2219.

window_2.governing: CAB-2219 debt ledger; CAB-2242 debt credit; CAB-2236 idle decay
window_2.note: The carry decays across the 200ms idle gap before it is credited. Note that debt_adjusted_dispatchable_ms is 297, not 296: the credit applied to 234 rounds in the direction CAB-2242 specifies, and a reader who rounds it the other way lands one millisecond low here and on every later window in the service.
